content.text:
<plugin_info kind="matched_installed">
display_name: Figma
description: Plugin that includes the Figma MCP server and Skills for common workflows
skills:
  - figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
  - figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
  - figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
  - figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
  - figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
  - figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
  - figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
  - figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
mcp_servers:
  - figma
</plugin_info>

/add-plugin figma
</user_query>

Summarizing the Figma plugin and checking the MCP server’s authentication status.

[REDACTED]

Triggering Figma MCP authentication so tools work after install.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what **adding the Figma plugin** gives you in Cursor:

## MCP server: **figma**

Runs Figma-related actions through the MCP bridge (e.g. **`use_figma`** for script-style work inside a file). **`STATUS.md`** said auth was required; **`mcp_auth`** was run and **authentication completed** for this workspace, so those tools should be usable now.

## Skills (use **`/`** in chat or rely on the agent)

| Skill | Role |
|--------|------|
| **`figma-use`** | **Required before every `use_figma` call** — how to run plugin API code safely and avoid common failures. |
| **`figma-create-new-file`** | **Required before `create_new_file`** — new Design / FigJam / Slides file. |
| **`figma-generate-diagram`** | **Required before `generate_diagram`** — flowcharts, ERDs, sequences, etc. (with Mermaid constraints). |
| **`figma-generate-design`** | Page-level “code → Figma” or “match this screen in Figma” workflows; pair with **`figma-use`**. |
| **`figma-generate-library`** | Tokens, components, theming from a codebase; pair with **`figma-use`**. |
| **`figma-code-connect`** | Code Connect mappings (`.figma.ts` / `.figma.js`). |
| **`figma-use-figjam`** / **`figma-use-slides`** | Extra context when using **`use_figma`** in FigJam or Slides. |

## Commands

Slash commands like **`/figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard`** map to those workflows (see the skill descriptions for exact usage).

## Automation

**Rules** and **hooks** from the plugin apply when configured; the agent can also pick skills automatically when your request matches their triggers.

---

**Next step for you:** open a Figma file (or FigJam/Slides) in the desktop app and keep the plugin/MCP session connected so **`use_figma`** runs in the right file context. If anything still fails, say what you tried (e.g. “create frame from code”) and we can trace it.

[REDACTED]

Here’s what blocked a true **1:1** pass and what we know from the repo.

## Figma access

Cursor’s Figma MCP **cannot read** [your file](https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-67&m=dev) (`get_design_context`, `get_screenshot`, and `use_figma` all return “file could not be accessed”). `whoami` shows you’re on a **Starter** plan with a **View** seat, which also limits how much design data MCP can pull; see Figma’s [rate limits & access](https://www.figma.com/developers/docs) guidance (same ideas as the MCP `rate-limits-access` resource).

**To unblock pixel work:** invite **`debojyoti.biswas@mail.mcgill.ca`** to the file (at least **can view**), and for reliable MCP reads consider a **Dev** or **Full** seat. Then we can re-run `get_design_context` on node **`1:67`** (from `node-id=1-67`).

## What the Discover code is doing today

`app/index.tsx` is already built as a **detailed Figma trace**, but it documents the **old** screen node **`1:38`**, not **`1:67`**. Your link points at **`1:67`** in a **different file**—in a duplicated doc, that frame is often the same screen with a new id, but it can also be a **child** (e.g. only header or grid). Without the file we can’t tell which.

Relevant pieces:

- **Layout:** safe area, background SVG polygon, scroll, header (“Choose Your Bike”), hero (`HeroBackground` + image + “30% Off”), horizontal categories, gradient strip + two-column masonry (`ProductCard` + `CardBackground`), `BottomTabBar`.
- **Scaling:** mix of `width / 390`, `sidePad`, `hs`, and fixed `StyleSheet` numbers—so not every token is scaled the same way; that may or may not match how **`1:67`** is set up in Dev Mode.

## What I need from you to “implement 1:1”

Pick one:

1. **Fix access** (invite + seat) and tell me when done—I’ll call design context on **`1:67`** and align `index.tsx` / related components, **or**
2. **Paste** Dev Mode for `1:67` (frame size, padding, typography, colors, spacing between header / hero / categories / grid, and any component that differs), **or** a **screenshot** of `1:67` with the **layers list** visible so we know scope (whole screen vs section).

I’m **not** changing the UI blindly: without that file or measurements, any “1:1” edits would be guesses and could move you **away** from your Figma frame.

content.text:
Here’s what was done.

## Figma MCP

The file from your link ([Design To Ship (Copy) — node `1-38`](https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-38&m=dev)) still **isn’t readable** via Figma MCP (access / seat limits), so this pass follows the **same node `1:38` layout already encoded in the repo** and the **390pt baseline** rules in `CLAUDE.md`.

## Code updates

1. **`app/index.tsx` (Discover)**  
   - Doc block now points at your Figma URL and node **`1-38`**.  
   - **`scale = width / 390`** drives spacing and type: top padding, header gap, title size/line height, search control (44pt, radius, icon 20pt, shadows), hero bottom margin, “30% Off” size, category strip, stagger (10pt steps, 16pt gap), bottom scroll inset.  
   - Horizontal inset **`Math.max(16, 20 * scale)`** and column gap **`20 * scale`** so insets match a **20pt** Figma margin at 390 and scale with width.  
   - Background polygon accent uses **`GRADIENTS.accent`** so it matches the rest of the app tokens (`#34C8E8` → `#4E4AF2`).

2. **`components/ProductCard.tsx`**  
   - Column width uses the **same** `sidePad` / `colGap` math as Discover so cards line up with the grid.

3. **`components/CategoryPill.tsx`**  
   - Pills, icons (28pt base), labels (13pt), radii, and shadow offsets/blur **scale with `width / 390`** so the category row matches the frame on mini vs Max.

`npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

---

**Note:** Running `npx expo lint` triggered Expo’s **first-time ESLint setup** (extra packages / config). That’s unrelated to the Discover edits; review `package.json` / lockfile if you want to keep or undo that.

When MCP can read your file (file shared to your account + suitable seat), we can diff **exact** Dev Mode numbers and tighten anything still off.

[REDACTED]

- **`npx expo lint`:** completed with **0 errors** and **6 warnings** (existing `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` in `detail.tsx`, `BottomTabBar.tsx`, `ProductCard.tsx`; unused vars in `CheckoutSlider.tsx`). First run also **initialized ESLint** in the project (installed `eslint` / `eslint-config-expo`).

**Follow-up:** Nothing required for the Discover work—the build type-checks. Optionally you can clean up those lint warnings or adjust the new ESLint setup if you didn’t want packages added.

<user_query>
expo project is loading on my phone when i scan the qr code
</user_query>

That’s expected when things are set up correctly: Expo Go reads the QR code, downloads the JS bundle, and then your app should open.

**If it finishes and you see the app** — you’re good; the first load is often the slowest.

**If it stays on “Opening project…” or “Downloading…” for a long time:**

- Phone and PC on the **same Wi‑Fi** (or use **Tunnel** in the Expo dev tools if corporate/guest networks block LAN).
- In the terminal running `npx expo start`, try **`s`** to switch connection type, or start with `npx expo start --tunnel` if LAN is flaky.
- **Close and reopen Expo Go**, then scan again.
- **Clear Metro cache** if it’s stuck: `npx expo start -c`.

If something specific is wrong (error screen, infinite loading, red box), paste the message or a screenshot and we can narrow it down.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was wrong and what to do next.

## Fix applied

**`expo-constants` was missing** even though `expo-router` needs it. That can break or destabilize the app in Expo Go. It’s now installed (`npx expo install expo-constants`), and **`npx expo-doctor` reports 17/17 checks passed.**

Restart the dev server and try again:

```bash
npx expo start -c
```

Then scan the QR code again (or reload in Expo Go).

---

## If it still fails

1. **Note what you see** — e.g. “Couldn’t connect to server”, “Something went wrong”, red error screen, or endless loading. That determines the next step.

2. **Expo Go version** — Install the latest **Expo Go** from the App Store / Play Store. This project is **SDK 52**; an old Expo Go often shows incompatibility errors.

3. **Network** — Phone and PC must be on the **same Wi‑Fi**, or use tunnel mode:

   ```bash
   npx expo start --tunnel
   ```

   (Slower, but works when LAN / firewall blocks the phone.)

4. **Windows firewall** — If Metro starts but the phone never loads, allow **Node** through the firewall when Windows prompts, or temporarily try with the firewall off to test.

5. **New Architecture** — Your `app.json` has `"newArchEnabled": true`. If problems continue only in Expo Go, set it to **`false`**, save, run `npx expo start -c` again, and retry.

Reply with the **exact message or screenshot** from the phone if it’s still not working after a clean start with `expo-constants` installed.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Clear .expo cache and run Metro via expo export to verify no ENOENT

content.text:
Here’s what was going on and what we did.

## What the error means

**`ENOENT … watch '…\@tybys\wasm-util\dist'`** means Metro’s file watcher tried to watch a folder that **wasn’t there**. That usually comes from:

1. A **broken or half-installed `node_modules`** (install interrupted, antivirus, optional deps), or  
2. **Stale Metro / Expo cache** still pointing at an old path after packages changed.

`@tybys/wasm-util` is pulled in as an **optional** dependency (via `@napi-rs/wasm-runtime`). On your machine it wasn’t present at all after a normal install, but Metro was still trying to watch the old path—classic **cache + bad install** combo.

## What we did

1. **Removed `node_modules` completely** and ran a fresh **`npm install`**.  
2. **Removed the `.expo` folder** so old dev-server state isn’t reused.  
3. Confirmed Metro runs cleanly with **`npx expo export --platform android`** (bundle completed, no `ENOENT`).

## What you should run

From the project folder:

```powershell
npx expo start -c
```

(`-c` clears Metro’s cache.) Then scan the QR code again.

If **`ENOENT` on `wasm-util` ever comes back**, repeat: delete `node_modules`, run `npm install`, delete `.expo`, then `npx expo start -c`. Keeping the project **outside** aggressive antivirus “real-time” scanning of `node_modules` also reduces random broken installs on Windows.

[REDACTED]

[Image]
<image_files>
The following images were provdied by the user and saved to the workspace for future use:
1. C:\Users\debob\.cursor\projects\c-Users-debob-Desktop-GymBros-TakeHome/assets/c__Users_debob_AppData_Roaming_Cursor_User_workspaceStorage_8b621341f2c699d0d37aa59eb35d31d3_images_image-f6186774-80a5-45f5-8436-755a3248d4c7.png

These images can be copied for use in other locations.
</image_files>
<user_query>

</user_query>

That message almost always means **Expo Go can’t reach your dev server** to download the JS bundle.

Your screenshot shows **5G** in the status bar. If your **PC is on Wi‑Fi** and Expo is using a **LAN** URL (`192.168.x.x`), the phone **on cellular only** usually **cannot** hit that address, so the download fails.

### Fix (pick one)

**A — Same network (simplest)**  
1. Turn **Wi‑Fi on** on the phone.  
2. Connect to the **same Wi‑Fi** as your computer.  
3. Stop the server, start again: `npx expo start -c`  
4. Scan the QR code again (or use the link in the terminal).

**B — Stay on cellular / different networks**  
Start with a **tunnel** so traffic goes through the internet, not your LAN:

```powershell
cd "c:\Users\debob\Desktop\GymBros TakeHome"
npx expo start --tunnel
```

First run may ask you to install `@expo/ngrok` / log in—follow the prompts. Then scan the new QR code.

**C — PC firewall**  
If you’re already on the same Wi‑Fi and it still fails, allow **Node.js** (or **Expo**) through **Windows Defender Firewall** for **Private** networks.

---

**Summary:** LAN QR + phone on **5G only** → connection fails. Use **same Wi‑Fi** or **`--tunnel`**.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
help me fix the mcp connect
</user_query>

Checking your workspace MCP setup and Figma server status.

[REDACTED]

Here’s a focused checklist. Your **Figma MCP is responding** from here (`whoami` works for `debojyoti.biswas@mail.mcgill.ca`), so “broken” is usually **Cursor UI**, **auth**, **Figma desktop**, or **which server** you mean.

---

### 1. **Cursor — MCP server list**

1. **Settings → MCP** (or **Cursor Settings → Features → MCP**).
2. Find **Figma** (or `plugin-figma-figma` / `figma`).
3. If it’s red / “Failed to connect”:
   - Turn the server **off**, wait a few seconds, **on** again.
   - **Reload Window**: Command Palette → `Developer: Reload Window`.

---

### 2. **Re-authenticate Figma**

In chat you can ask the agent to run auth, or in Cursor use whatever control the Figma MCP exposes to **Sign in / Connect** again.

If tools exist but **files return “could not be accessed”**:

- Log into **the same Figma account** in the browser and in the **Figma desktop app** as the one you authorized for MCP.
- **Invite that account** to the file (Viewer is enough for read in many cases, but your **Starter + View** seat still has **very low MCP read quotas** — upgrading seat/plan may be required for steady access).

---

### 3. **Figma desktop (for `use_figma` / live file)**

Figma’s own doc says if tools don’t load or the connection feels dead:

1. Open the file in **Figma desktop** (not only browser).
2. Switch to **Dev Mode**.
3. In the **MCP** section, ensure the **Dev Mode MCP server** is **enabled**.
4. Keep that **file open** while using MCP.
5. **Quit Figma fully** and reopen, then **reload Cursor** if it still fails.

Remote tools like `whoami` can work while **design read** still needs permissions / quota / correct file.

---

### 4. **If you meant **Dart** MCP** (`mcp.json`)**

Your global `C:\Users\debob\.cursor\mcp.json` only defines **Dart**:

```json
"command": "dart mcp-server --experimental-mcp-server --force-roots-fallback"
```

If that server won’t connect:

- Install the **Dart SDK** and ensure `dart` is on your **PATH** (open a new terminal and run `dart --version`).
- In Cursor MCP settings, check the error text (often “spawn dart ENOENT” = PATH issue).

---

### 5. **Still stuck**

Reply with **exactly** what you see, for example:

- “Figma MCP: **Disconnected**” in Settings, or  
- Tools list empty, or  
- Red error string from MCP logs, or  
- Only **`get_design_context` fails** with “file could not be accessed”.

Different symptoms → different fixes; paste the **one line error** from Cursor’s MCP panel if you can.

## What already lines up well

| Area | Figma (`1:38`) | App |
|------|----------------|-----|
| Background | `#242c3b` | `COLORS.bg` |
| Hero card width / insets | Top card from **x = 20**, width **350** | `sidePad` ≈ 20 @ 390, `cardW` = full width − 2×pad |
| Bike image | **(37, 166)** size **316.818×153** in file space | `(17, 30)` × `(317, 153)` in card space — same relative placement |
| “30% Off” | **y = 313** from screen top, card top **136** → **177** below card top | `top: 177 * hs` |
| “30% Off” typography | Poppins Bold **26**, `rgba(255,255,255,0.6)` | `lx.promoSize` / `lx.promoLh`, same color |
| Title | Poppins Bold **20** white | `lx.titleSize` / `lx.titleLh` |
| Header control | **44×44** | `lx.searchBtn` = 44×scale |
| Grid | Two columns **165** wide, **20** gutter, left column **+31** | `colW`, `colGap`, `31 * scale` |
| Masonry / card content | Poppins **13 / 15 / 13**, muted labels | `ProductCard` styles |
| Tab bar | Slanted bar, pill, home indicator | `BottomTabBar` (already spec’d from same file) |

---

## Gaps / mismatches (worth fixing if you want pixel parity)

1. **Header icon (clear mismatch)**  
   Figma node **`1:44`** is named **`Cart Button`** and uses the cart asset.  
   The app uses **`SearchIcon`** but still navigates to the cart (`handleCartPress`).  
   **→** Swap to **`CartIcon`** (you already have it) so icon, name, and behavior match Figma.

2. **Active category pill size**  
   In Figma the highlighted **“All”** pill is **50×50** (same as inactive siblings in that row).  
   The app uses **48×48** for the **active** state and **50×50** for inactive (`CategoryPill.tsx`).  
   **→** Use **50×50** for active as well if you want 1:1 with `1:47`.

3. **Horizontal spacing between category pills**  
   Figma pill left edges: **30, 100, 170, 240, 310** → **70** between left edges, **50** wide pills → **20** px gap between columns.  
   The app uses **`pillGap` = 16 × scale**.  
   **→** Prefer **20 × scale** for `marginRight` between pills.

4. **Category row horizontal inset**  
   First pill starts at **x = 30** on the **390** frame (with content at **20** for nav), i.e. **+10** vs a simple `paddingHorizontal: 20` scroll.  
   **→** Optional: add **`paddingLeft: 10 * scale`** (or start content at **30**) on the category `ScrollView` so the row matches Figma’s **30** left edge.

5. **Status bar in the design**  
   The Figma frame includes a **decorative status bar** (`1:41`). The real app uses the **system** status bar + `SafeAreaView` — that’s expected; no need to duplicate the mock status bar unless you want a marketing screenshot build.

6. **Artboard `rounded-[50px]`**  
   The generated code wraps the screen in **`rounded-[50px]`** — that’s **device chrome in the file**, not something you’d normally ship in Expo. Your app correctly **does not** clip the whole screen to 50 radius.

---

## Summary

- For audits, use **[Discover `1:38`](https://www.figma.com/design/VYIhA7NSt2WzRJEzZNSeNY/Design-To-Ship--Copy---Copy-?node-id=1-38&m=dev)** — not `0-1`.  
- Overall layout, hero math, grid, typography, and tab bar are **very close** to Figma.  
- The main visible deltas are **cart vs search icon**, **active pill 48 vs 50**, **16 vs 20** pill spacing, and a **~10pt** category row inset tweak.

I can apply those four UI tweaks in the repo in a follow-up if you want them implemented, not just audited.

{/* Right column: reflect path across vertical midline (x′ = 205 − x) so slant faces the gutter */}

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong and what we changed.

### Cause

1. **“Clear” right cards** — In `CardBackground`, the **right** column used a different fill (`#363E51` → `#191E26`) and **`fillOpacity={0.6}`**, which was meant as a glass treatment. In your Figma frame, those cards should read like the left column: **solid** `#353F54` → `#222834`.

2. **Wrong slant / “flipped” feel** — The right column used a **second hand-built path** (mirrored control points). That path didn’t match a true **reflection** of the left Figma path, so the parallelogram could look twisted or flipped compared to Rectangle **167** vs **166**.

### Fix (`components/CardBackground.tsx`)

- **One path** — Always build the same **Rectangle 166** path as the left card.
- **Right column** — Wrap it in `<G transform="translate(205, 0) scale(-1, 1)">` so it’s a clean **reflection across the vertical midline** (`x′ = 205 − x` for the shape that runs from **20** to **185**).
- **Fill** — Both columns now use **`#353F54` → `#222834`** with **`fillOpacity={1}`** (no separate “glass” branch).

`ProductCard.tsx` only had a comment update to point at this behavior.

Reload the app on a device/simulator: right-column cards should look **opaque** like Figma and the **slant** should face the gutter correctly. If anything still looks off on a specific Android build (e.g. shadow + `G` transform), say which device/SDK and we can adjust filters vs. group order.

[REDACTED]

content.text:
Here’s what was going wrong in your screenshots and what we changed.

### What you were seeing

1. **Opposite “tilt”** — Left cards read as slanting one way and right cards the other. That came from **`translate(205,0) scale(-1,1)`** on the right column: a horizontal flip, which reads like mirrored perspective vs the left column.

2. **Right cards looking glassy / blue bleed** — In your **Figma** comp, both product tiles use the **same** slant (top edge runs **down left → right** on both sides). After our earlier “mirror” fix, **Android + `react-native-svg`** was still compositing the **filtered** path inside a **transformed `<G>`** oddly, so the big cyan polygon showed through more on the right.

### What we did

In `CardBackground.tsx` we **removed the mirror entirely**: **left and right use the same single `Path`** (the Rectangle 166 geometry), with the same opaque fill **`#353F54` → `#222834`**, **`fillOpacity={1}`**, and **`opacity={1}`** on the path.

So:

- Slant and “direction” of the card **match Figma on both columns**.
- No `<G transform="…">` + filter combo on the right, which should stop the **translucent / tinted** look over the blue wedge.

Rebuild / reload the app (`npx expo start -c` if needed). If any card still looks faint on a specific device, say which one—we can next try rasterizing that layer (`shouldRasterizeIOS` / Android equivalent) or simplifying the SVG shadow for that path.

content.text:
Here’s what was wrong and what we changed.

### 1. Top “shadow” on the grid  
That came from the **`LinearGradient`** (`COLORS.bg` → transparent) that sat **above** the masonry block (the old Rectangle 480 bridge). It isn’t part of the current Figma look you want, so **that gradient and its wrapper `View` are removed**.

### 2. Grid shifted left  
The scroll body didn’t have a fixed width, so on some devices the column row didn’t line up cleanly with the hero. Updates:

- **`contentContainerStyle`**: `width: width`, explicit `paddingLeft` / `paddingRight`, and **`alignItems: 'center'`** so children are centered in the scroll area.
- **Header**, **category row** (`ScrollView`), and **grid** each use **`width: cardW`** — same as the hero (`width − 2×sidePad`), matching Figma’s **350pt-wide** content band on a **390** frame.

So the hero, categories, and product grid share one width and sit centered under the same horizontal padding.

---

Reload the app; the dark band above the cards should be gone and the grid should line up with the hero. If anything still looks off on a specific device, say which model and we can tune safe-area width next.

content.text:
Updating `ProductCard`'s layout helper to match the new `cardW` / `colW` math.

[REDACTED]

input.description: Typecheck after layout and ProductCard edits

content.text:
Here’s what changed:

### 1. **Shadows under product images** (`ProductCard.tsx`)
Removed the oval “ground shadow” `View` under each product image (the bar under the bike/helmet). Dropped the unused `shadowW` entries from `LAYOUT`.

### 2. **Grid centered with equal side margins** (`app/index.tsx` + `ProductCard.tsx`)
- **Content width** is now **`cardW = min(350 × (width/390), width − 32)`** so it tracks the Figma **350pt** band on a **390pt** frame, with at least **16px** margin on each side on very narrow devices.
- **Column math** uses that same band: **`colW = (cardW − colGap) / 2`** with **`colGap = 20 × scale`**, so the two columns + gutter always fill **`cardW`** exactly.
- **Symmetry:** horizontal space is **`(width − cardW) / 2`** on the left and right by construction (no more `sidePad` vs `width` mismatch).
- **Scroll layout:** `contentContainerStyle` uses **`width` + `alignItems: 'center'`** and a single inner **`View`** with **`width: cardW`** wrapping header, hero, categories, and grid so the whole stack is centered the same way.

`discoverColLayout` in **`ProductCard.tsx`** was updated to use the same **`cardW` / `colW`** formulas so cards still line up with the grid.

Reload the app; the dark bars under images should be gone and the product block should sit centered with even side margins.

[REDACTED]

## Figma MCP

## Discrepancies (your screenshot + `app/cart.tsx` / `CheckoutSlider.tsx`)

### 1. **Header title alignment**
The header is a single row: **back** then **“My Shopping Cart”** with `gap: 16`. The title is **not** centered on the screen; it sits beside the back control. Many Figma carts use a **screen-centered title** with the back control absolutely on the left.

### 2. **“Checkout” label on the slider**
In `CheckoutSlider`, **“Checkout”** is positioned with `position: 'absolute', right: 20` (and the same for “Done!”). So the label sits on the **right** of the track. Your description matches a layout where **“Checkout” reads centered** in the pill with the thumb on the **left** — opposite emphasis.

### 3. **Currency formatting inconsistency**
- Line item prices use **`$ 1,999.99`** (space after `$`).
- `formatPrice` uses **`$4,283.99`** (no space) for subtotal/total.

Figma/dev mode usually picks one pattern; mixing them reads as off.

### 4. **Horizontal band / “left heavy” content**
Rows use **`paddingHorizontal: 20`**, but there is **no** shared centered **content width** (unlike Discover after the `cardW` wrapper). On wide devices the block can feel **stuck to the left** with more empty space on the right, similar to what you called out on Discover.

### 5. **Extra chrome vs Figma**
- **`homeIndicator`** at the bottom of the cart is **custom** (134×5). The real device already draws a home indicator; Figma may or may not include this — it can look like an extra bar.
- **`CheckoutSlider`** adds **“Done! ✓”** and green-ish accent when complete; the static Figma frame is usually only the default “Checkout” state.

### 6. **Shadows**
Back button, Apply, checkout track/thumb use **elevation / drop shadows**. Your note says shadows are visible on back and checkout. If the Figma frame is flat (no shadow tokens on those nodes), that’s a visual delta.

### 7. **Thumbnails**
Cart rows wrap the image in a **linear gradient** (`#363e51` → `#4c5770`). If Figma uses a **flat** image well or a different fill, color will differ slightly.

### 8. **Copy / data (not layout)**
`INITIAL_CART` uses **`PILOT - CHROMOLY 520`** and prices that drive **subtotal $6,119.98** and **30% off → total $4,283.99** — matches a “Bike30” scenario; only a discrepancy if Figma lists different line items or naming.

---

<user_query>
why is the figma mcp not connected
</user_query>

Here’s what that message usually means and what to check.

## What “not connected” meant in your session

When the agent called the Figma tools, Cursor responded with something like **`MCP server does not exist: plugin-figma-figma`** and **no MCP servers available**. So from **that chat’s point of view**, there was **no Figma MCP server registered** (or it failed to start), not that Figma rejected your login.

That’s **Cursor ↔ MCP wiring**, not your React/Expo repo.

## Typical causes

1. **Figma MCP turned off or removed**  
   In **Cursor Settings → MCP**, the Figma / `figma` entry might be disabled, deleted, or never added on this machine or profile.

2. **Wrong scope**  
   MCP can be configured **globally** vs **per project**. This workspace might not have the Figma server in its MCP config, while another project does.

3. **Server failed to start**  
   If the Figma MCP process crashes on launch (config error, network, etc.), Cursor may show no servers or omit that one until you fix the error (often a line in **MCP** logs / output).

4. **Session / reload**  
   Occasionally a reload or update leaves MCP unloaded until you **restart Cursor** or toggle the server off/on.

5. **Different Cursor build / channel**  
   MCP availability and server IDs can differ by version; an old or minimal install might not load marketplace MCPs the same way.

## What you can do

1. **Cursor → Settings → MCP**  
   - Confirm a **Figma** (or `figma` / plugin) server exists and is **enabled**.  
   - If there’s an error badge, open logs and fix what it says (path, auth, etc.).

2. **Re-add the Figma integration**  
   If it’s missing: install/enable the **Figma** plugin from Cursor’s integrations / marketplace so it registers the MCP server again.

3. **Restart Cursor** after changing MCP config.

4. **Figma app + auth (for design tools)**  
   Even when MCP is “connected,” **reading files** still needs you to be logged into the right Figma account and to have access to the file. That’s separate from “server not found.”

I can’t see your Cursor UI from here, so the exact reason is whichever of the above matches **your** MCP panel (empty list vs disabled Figma vs startup error). If you paste a screenshot or the exact MCP error line from Cursor, we can narrow it to one cause.
